title: Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.29: Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.29' running on index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary or sourcetype=tenable:plugin, patch_evaluation_register.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary earliest=-35d classification="Critical"
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| join type=left kb_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation kb_id=* earliest=-35d
    | stats latest(evaluated_at) as evaluated_at by kb_id
  ]
| where isnull(evaluated_at)
| stats dc(host) as hosts_missing_eval by kb_id, title
description: We track that each published critical patch for BES assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc, patch and sourcetypes cip:patch_evaluation, tenable:plugin`, wsus:summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest patch catalog with release dates. (2) Require evaluation HEC event per KB. (3) Alert before internal evaluation SLA. (4) Export for CIP-007 evidence binders. Indexes required: `index=grc`, `index=patch`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation`, `sourcetype=tenable:plugin``, `sourcetype=wsus:summary`, `sourcetype=wsus:summary``; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.
visualization: Table (unevaluated KBs), Single value (count), Time chart (evaluations). Key fields: `evaluated_at`, `hosts_missing_eval`, `kb_id`, `title`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we track that each published critical patch for bes assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=patch` `sourcetype=wsus:summary` or `sourcetype=tenable:plugin`, `patch_evaluation_register.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest patch catalog with release dates. (2) Require evaluation HEC event per KB. (3) Alert before internal evaluation SLA. (4) Export for CIP-007 evidence binders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary earliest=-35d classification="Critical"
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| join type=left kb_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation kb_id=* earliest=-35d
    | stats latest(evaluated_at) as evaluated_at by kb_id
  ]
| where isnull(evaluated_at)
| stats dc(host) as hosts_missing_eval by kb_id, title
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking (CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.1)** — We track that each published critical patch for BES assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=patch` `sourcetype=wsus:summary` or `sourcetype=tenable:plugin`, `patch_evaluation_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: patch; **sourcetype**: wsus:summary. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=patch, sourcetype=wsus:summary, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(evaluated_at)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **kb_id, title**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.

Output fields: evaluated_at, hosts_missing_eval, kb_id, title.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unevaluated KBs), Single value (count), Time chart (evaluations)
